likely_response.local: Siga as placas para a Plataforma 2.
likely_response.pronunciation: SEE-guh UHSH PLAH-kuhsh PUH-ruh AH pluh-tuh-FAW-ruh-muh DOYSH.

responses.phonetic: SEE-guh UHSH PLAH-kuhsh PUH-ruh AH pluh-tuh-FAW-ruh-muh DOYSH.
responses.local: Siga as placas para a Plataforma 2.

phrases.text_local: Onde ficam os validadores de bilhetes?
phrases.pronunciation: OHNG-dih FEE-kuhng OOSH vuh-lee-duh-DOH-rihsh DIH bee-LYEH-tihsh?
phrases.cultural_note: In Portuguese stations, ticket validation is mandatory before boarding regional trains.

likely_response.local: Bom dia, junto às catracas de acesso.
likely_response.pronunciation: BOHNG DEEUH, ZHOONNG-too UHUHSH kuht-RAH-kuhsh DIH uh-SEH-soo.
likely_response.translation_en: Good morning, near the access turnstiles.

responses.english: Good morning, near the access turnstiles.
responses.phonetic: BOHNG DEEUH, ZHOONNG-too UHUHSH kuht-RAH-kuhsh DIH uh-SEH-soo.
responses.local: Bom dia, junto às catracas de acesso.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the self-service machines are
phrases.text_local: Onde estão as máquinas de autoatendimento?
phrases.pronunciation: OHNG-dih ihsh-TOWN UHSH MAH-kee-nuhsh DIH ow-toouh-tayng-dee-MAYNG-too?
phrases.cultural_note: Self-service machines are standard at CP stations for quick ticket purchases.

likely_response.local: Bom dia, lado oposto à bilheteira.
likely_response.pronunciation: BOHNG DEEUH, LAH-doo aw-POHSH-too AH bee-lyih-TAY-ruh.
likely_response.translation_en: Good morning, opposite the ticket office.

responses.english: Good morning, opposite the ticket office.
responses.phonetic: BOHNG DEEUH, LAH-doo aw-POHSH-too AH bee-lyih-TAY-ruh.
responses.local: Bom dia, lado oposto à bilheteira.

phrases.text_local: Onde posso validar o bilhete na plataforma?
phrases.pronunciation: OHNG-dih PAW-soo vuh-lee-DAHR AW bee-LYEH-tih NUH pluh-tuh-FAW-ruh-muh?
phrases.cultural_note: Platform validators are often mounted on concrete pillars near the boarding area.

likely_response.local: Bom dia, nas caixas de validação nas plataformas.
likely_response.pronunciation: BOHNG DEEUH, NUHSH KEYE-shuhsh DIH vuh-lee-duh-SOWN NUHSH pluh-tuh-FOH-ruh-muhsh.
likely_response.translation_en: Good morning, at the validation boxes on the platforms.

responses.english: Good morning, at the validation boxes on the platforms.
responses.phonetic: BOHNG DEEUH, NUHSH KEYE-shuhsh DIH vuh-lee-duh-SOWN NUHSH pluh-tuh-FOH-ruh-muhsh.
responses.local: Bom dia, nas caixas de validação nas plataformas.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the level crossing is
phrases.text_local: Onde fica a passagem de nível?
phrases.pronunciation: OHNG-dih FEE-kuh AH puh-SAH-zhayng DIH NEE-vehl?
phrases.cultural_note: Level crossings are common in smaller Portuguese stations for pedestrian track access.

likely_response.local: Siga as placas para a passagem de nível.
likely_response.pronunciation: SEE-guh UHSH PLAH-kuhsh PUH-ruh AH puh-SAH-zhayng DIH NEE-vehl.
likely_response.translation_en: Follow the signs to the level crossing.

responses.english: Follow the signs to the level crossing.
responses.phonetic: SEE-guh UHSH PLAH-kuhsh PUH-ruh AH puh-SAH-zhayng DIH NEE-vehl.
responses.local: Siga as placas para a passagem de nível.

phrases.intent_en: Ask which platform for Faro trains
phrases.text_local: Qual a plataforma para o Faro?
phrases.pronunciation: KWAHL AH pluh-tuh-FAW-ruh-muh PUH-ruh AW FAH-roo?
phrases.cultural_note: Faro is the main regional hub, often sharing platforms 1 and 2.

likely_response.local: Bom dia, utilize a plataforma um ou dois.
likely_response.pronunciation: BOHNG DEEUH, oo-tee-LEE-zih AH pluh-tuh-FAW-ruh-muh OONNG OHW DOYSH.
likely_response.translation_en: Good morning, use platform one or two.

responses.english: Good morning, use platform one or two.
responses.phonetic: BOHNG DEEUH, oo-tee-LEE-zih AH pluh-tuh-FAW-ruh-muh OONNG OHW DOYSH.
responses.local: Bom dia, utilize a plataforma um ou dois.

phrases.text_local: Scusi, onde fica a plataforma dois?
phrases.pronunciation: skoo-ZEE, OHNG-dih FEE-kuh AH pluh-tuh-FAW-ruh-muh DOYSH?
phrases.cultural_note: In Portuguese train stations, platforms are often referred to by number; 'plataforma dois' is standard.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the ticket office is
phrases.text_local: Onde é a bilheteira da CP?
phrases.pronunciation: OHNG-dih EH AH bee-lyih-TAY-ruh DUH seh-PEH?
phrases.cultural_note: CP refers to Comboios de Portugal, the national railway operator.

likely_response.local: Siga as placas para a bilheteira.
likely_response.pronunciation: SEE-guh UHSH PLAH-kuhsh PUH-ruh AH bee-lyih-TAY-ruh.
likely_response.translation_en: Follow the signs to the ticket office.

responses.english: Follow the signs to the ticket office.
responses.phonetic: SEE-guh UHSH PLAH-kuhsh PUH-ruh AH bee-lyih-TAY-ruh.
responses.local: Siga as placas para a bilheteira.

phrases.cultural_note: In Portugal, ticket validation is required before boarding. Look for the yellow validation machines on the platforms.

likely_response.local: Bom dia, nas caixas de validação das plataformas.
likely_response.pronunciation: BOHNG DEEUH, NUHSH KEYE-shuhsh DIH vuh-lee-duh-SOWN DUHSH pluh-tuh-FOH-ruh-muhsh.
likely_response.translation_en: Good morning, at the validation boxes on the platforms.

responses.english: Good morning, at the validation boxes on the platforms.
responses.phonetic: BOHNG DEEUH, NUHSH KEYE-shuhsh DIH vuh-lee-duh-SOWN DUHSH pluh-tuh-FOH-ruh-muhsh.
responses.local: Bom dia, nas caixas de validação das plataformas.
